title: Magnetic or Non-Magnetic?
curriculum: NCERT Grade 6 · Science · Magnetic and Non-magnetic Materials
description: Interactive drag-the-magnet sorting game. One everyday object sits on the table at a time; the student drags (or taps) a single horseshoe magnet onto it to test it. Iron and steel objects leap up and cling to the poles; everything else only wobbles and stays put. Each tested object drops into a Magnetic or Non-magnetic tray, and once all objects are tested the game shows a two-column Magnetic vs Non-magnetic results table with a replay button.

placement.where: Replaces the classroom bar-magnet demo. The student tests each everyday object with the magnet and sorts it; the agent guides with highlight() and reads the emitted events. In ai_operated mode the agent can demonstrate by calling testObject().

studentControls.control: Drag the horseshoe magnet down onto the object
studentControls.effect: tests the object; magnetic ones leap up and cling to the poles, non-magnetic ones only wobble
auto-moves the magnet onto it and tests it (mobile / accessibility friendly)

studentControls.control: Tap 'Play again' on the results screen
studentControls.effect: reshuffles the objects and restarts the game

answerKey.operatorModesNote: ai = collapse to the focused demo view (draggable magnet + student hint hidden, 'watch' caption shown, agent drives via testObject()); student = full interactive controls. Default 'student'.
answerKey.note: The object being tested is always the current one; whether it clings is fixed by its 'magnetic' membership above — testObject() reveals the truth, the student cannot get a 'wrong' answer.

eventDetails.item_placed: detail: { itemId, name, material, magnetic:boolean, tray:'magnetic'|'nonmagnetic' } — emitted each time an object is tested and sorted.
eventDetails.highlighted: detail: { target } — emitted when the agent points at a named element.
eventDetails.completed: detail: { total, magnetic:string[], nonmagnetic:string[] } — emitted when every object has been tested and the results table appears.
eventDetails.reset: detail: { total } — emitted when the game restarts.

scientificFacts: A magnet attracts only some materials — these are called magnetic materials.
Iron, steel, nickel and cobalt are magnetic; they leap towards and cling to a magnet.
Materials like glass, wood, plastic, rubber, copper, aluminium and brass are non-magnetic — a magnet does not attract them.
Steel is mostly iron, which is why steel spoons, pins and scissors are attracted to a magnet.

gradeLevel.description: Tunes the celebration tier on the results screen: confetti amount, size of the 'Sorted!' heading, and replay wording (6 = 'Play again!', most playful; 7 = 'Play again'; 8 = 'Try again', most restrained). May also be supplied inside data.gradeLevel; the top-level prop wins.

operatorMode.description: Host/platform-set. 'ai' collapses to the demo view (controls hidden, watch caption shown); 'student' shows the full interactive panel. Settable via prop, setParam('operatorMode', …) or setOperatorMode(mode).

muted.description: Mutes the Web Audio cues. Also toggled by setParam('muted', boolean) and the in-header mute button.

seed.description: Seeds the deterministic shuffle — the same seed reproduces the same test order. Supply via props.seed or additionalProps.seed; settable via setParam('seed', n) before reset().

data.description: Tool configuration merged over the built-in defaults via {...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...data}. See the 'data' schema below.
Configuration merged over the defaults. Pass only the fields you want to change.

title.description: Main heading shown in the gradient header.

subtitle.default: Test each object with the magnet — which ones cling?

instruction.default: Drag the magnet down onto each object. Magnetic ones leap up and cling to the poles!
instruction.description: Instruction line shown above the play board (student mode). In ai mode a 'watch' caption is shown instead.

gradeLevel.description: Celebration tier. The top-level gradeLevel prop, if provided, overrides this.

objects.description: The pool of objects the student tests. Reshuffled into a well-mixed order (no long magnetic/non-magnetic runs) at the start of every game. Whether an object clings is driven solely by its 'magnetic' flag.

id.description: Unique identifier within the list (also the arg id used by highlight/answerKey).

name.description: Display name shown under the object and in the trays/results table.

magnetic.description: true = leaps up and clings; false = only wobbles and is sorted as non-magnetic.

iconKey.description: Which built-in hand-drawn artwork to render. Unknown keys fall back to a plain gray circle.

quick_round.title: Will it stick?
quick_round.instruction: Drag the magnet onto each object to test it.

grade_8_restrained.title: Magnetic Materials Test
grade_8_restrained.instruction: Test each object with the magnet and record whether it is attracted.

iconKeys.description: Built-in hand-drawn SVG artwork keys available for object.iconKey. No external images needed.
iconKeys.note: iconKey only chooses the picture; the magnetic flag alone decides behaviour.

agentUsage.ai_operated: Set operatorMode 'ai' (controls hidden, 'watch' caption shown). Call testObject() to demonstrate testing the current object; narrate the item_placed event. Use highlight('object') / highlight('magneticTray') / highlight('nonMagneticTray') to point while explaining. reset() reshuffles and restarts.
agentUsage.student_operated: Set operatorMode 'student' (default). The student drags or taps to test each object; watch the item_placed and completed events and coach. Use highlight(target) to nudge toward the magnet or a tray without doing the action.
